name: Anthropomorphic Object Drama
description: Pixar-3D telenovela parody played dead-straight by anthropomorphic objects — fruit, vegetables, household appliances, anything with a face-on-object look. 7×8s = 56s default, structured as hook → setup → rising → discovery → confrontation → departure → resolution. Direct on-camera dialogue (one or two speakers per beat, never a narrator) carried by Veo 3.1 native audio. Single continuous music bed with a two-section emotional arc: warm pastel orchestral-pop opening + dramatic strings swell at the confrontation beat (~sec 32). Popping-word captions overlay one word at a time at the emotional peak. Beat-locked-to-dialogue: every 8s scene = exactly one dialogue beat in the story arc, every keyframe seeds the i2v anchor for that beat, every caption pop fires on the strongest word of the line.

stackSummary.keyframes: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter — one keyframe per scene as the i2v first-frame anchor. Scene-01 keyframe doubles as the cast-lineup master (all main characters in frame) so later keyframes can match identity.
stackSummary.i2v: google/veo-3.1 via OpenRouter — 8s clips, native audio ON, --first-frame = scene keyframe. English dialogue is the validated-clean path; Russian works but drifts (per MODELS.md). Every prompt restates the full lock-string of every character in frame because Veo has no character-lock primitive.
stackSummary.voiceover: NONE separately — Veo native audio IS the on-camera dialogue. Do not stack ElevenLabs TTS on top; lip-sync would desync.
stackSummary.music: ElevenLabs Music — ONE continuous bed, ~duration of full video, with internal emotional arc (warm opening + swell at confrontation beat). Two structural moments inside ONE stem, not two separate stems. Mixed under VO at ~-22 LUFS via ffmpeg sidechain-compress so dialogue stays intelligible.
stackSummary.captions: ElevenLabs scribe_v1 word-level on the final mixed audio → popping-word overlay (one word at a time, spring scale-in on emotional peak, ~700ms hold, ~100ms fade). White fill, heavy black stroke, Inter 900 uppercase, anchored bottom-center.
stackSummary.composition: Custom per-project Remotion (this template ships no generic composition). Consumer hand-authors src/videos/<id>/ — concat 7 clips, overlay music bed at -22 LUFS sidechained, burn popping-word captions on top.

constants.vibeAnchor: Pixar-3D anthropomorphic object cast acting out a dead-serious telenovela in 7 beats of 8s each
constants.characterStyle: face-on-object — a single object (fruit / vegetable / appliance / produce) IS the head, mounted on a humanoid body in simple clothing. Smooth glossy 3D textures, big expressive eyes, no photoreal humans, no hybrid creatures.
constants.captionStyle: popping-word — Inter 900 uppercase, white fill #FFFFFF + 5px black stroke #000000, ~104px, anchored bottom-center y=1380, fires on emotional peak word, 700ms hold + 100ms fade
constants.musicPolicy: ONE bed with two-section emotional arc (warm intro + climax swell at the confrontation beat). NOT two separate stems — single continuous file. Veo prompts MUST ban background music inside the clip ('no music' clause) so the only music is the editor-stage overlay.
constants.structurePolicy: beat-locked-to-dialogue — every 8s scene = exactly one dialogue beat. Hook (sc-01) → setup (sc-02) → rising (sc-03) → discovery (sc-04) → confrontation (sc-05, music swell) → departure (sc-06) → resolution (sc-07).

variationAxes: object class — fruit / vegetable / household appliance / kitchen gadget / packaged goods / office supplies / tools
plot trope — affair / breakup / found-family / love-triangle / mistaken-identity / parent-child / glow-up / rivalry
tone — melodrama played straight (default) / deadpan / soap-opera serious
VO language — English (validated-clean, default) / Russian (works but drifts) / Spanish / Mandarin
duration — 6×8s = 48s to 8×8s = 64s; 7×8s = 56s is the sweet spot
music arc — orchestral-pop (default) / acoustic-warm / synth-melancholic — always single bed with one swell at confrontation

slots.defaultExample: a Pixar-3D cartoon banana-headed man, smooth glossy yellow banana peel head with brown ripe spots, friendly round black eyes, small mouth, wearing a slightly rumpled white office shirt with rolled sleeves and a loose navy tie, beige trousers, mid-30s nervous family-man vibe

a Pixar-3D cartoon strawberry-headed woman, glossy red strawberry head with tiny yellow seeds, two small green leaves on top like hair-bow, big trusting brown eyes with long lashes, soft pink cheeks, wearing a pastel cream apron over a sky-blue summer dress, gentle motherly posture

a Pixar-3D cartoon woman with one single glossy bright red cherry as her head, long curled green stem on top, sharp confident half-lidded green eyes, deep red glossy lips, wearing a fitted black blazer with a low-cut white blouse, red pencil skirt, gold pendant, predatory warm smile

a Pixar-3D cartoon orange carrot-headed man, smooth tapered bright orange carrot head with three tall green leaves on top like a fauxhawk, chiseled jawline, blue aviator sunglasses, brown leather biker jacket, white t-shirt, confident relaxed swagger, late-30s bad-boy-with-heart

orchestral-pop dramatic-comedy — soft piano + light pizzicato strings opening, dramatic swelling strings + sharp staccato accents on the betrayal beat at ~32s, warm gentle hopeful piano resolution at ~48s

doNotCopyLiterally: Cast (banana / strawberry / cherry / carrot), dialogue lines, exact scene settings, exact prompts. The reusable layer is the structural pattern (7 beats × 8s, beat-locked-to-dialogue, single keyframe → veo i2v, single music bed with two-section arc, popping-word captions) and the prompt scaffolding around lock-strings + on-camera-dialogue language locks. Pick a different object class + cast + plot trope every time.
referenceNotes: No user reference required by default — cast is generated from prompt lock-strings of objects. If the user names a real brand, branded mascot, or specific real person (e.g. 'make Heinz ketchup the wife'), escalate to AGENTS.md hard rule #3 (reference-required gate) and refuse without a user-supplied reference image.

anchorMasterCastLineup.note: Reference example of the scene-01 cast-lineup keyframe pattern from fruit-drama-001. Pixar-3D banana-husband + strawberry-wife in a warm kitchen doorway. Use as a stylistic anchor when prompting your own scene-01 keyframe — match the framing (warm pastel two-shot), glossy-3D render quality, and 'first-frame establishes both leads' rule. Not for direct copy; regenerate with your own cast.

musicBedOrchestralPopDramaticComedy.note: Reusable orchestral-pop dramatic-comedy bed from fruit-drama-001 — 56s, single continuous arc with warm pastel opening + dramatic swell at ~32s confrontation beat + gentle resolution at ~48s. Drop-in if your scenario also lands a 56s 7-beat arc with the betrayal beat on scene-05. Re-prompt ElevenLabs Music if your duration / arc differ.
